{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.3,
    "R1.2": 0.7,
    "R1.3": 0.0,
    "R1.4": 0.3,
    "R1.5": 0.3,
    "R1.6": 1.0,
    "R1.7": 0.0,
    "RX.1": 0.7,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 0.7,
    "RX.5": 0.7
  },
  "active_set": ["R1.2", "R1.6", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should the review focus on (A) sentence-level writing polish and clarity, (B) high-level framing and logical coherence of the claims, or (C) both with lighter depth on each?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "A quick review should verify the coherence of the argument chain in an abstract-style paragraph claiming contributions.",
    "R1.6": "Given the request to look at a paragraph, sentence-level clarity, flow, and conciseness are likely the primary need.",
    "RX.1": "Any feedback should note limits due to assessing only a brief excerpt and not the full paper.",
    "RX.2": "We must avoid endorsing strong novelty or coverage claims without verification.",
    "RX.3": "The response should stay within the scope of reviewing this paragraph rather than broader experimentation or literature audit.",
    "RX.4": "Edits or guidance should not distort causal claims or encourage misuse in applied settings.",
    "RX.5": "Observations should be tied to specific phrases/sentences so the author can trace suggestions back to the source text."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Should the review focus on (A) sentence-level writing polish and clarity, (B) high-level framing and logical coherence of the claims, or (C) both with lighter depth on each?

rationale.R1.2: A quick review should verify the coherence of the argument chain in an abstract-style paragraph claiming contributions.
rationale.R1.6: Given the request to look at a paragraph, sentence-level clarity, flow, and conciseness are likely the primary need.
rationale.RX.1: Any feedback should note limits due to assessing only a brief excerpt and not the full paper.
rationale.RX.2: We must avoid endorsing strong novelty or coverage claims without verification.
rationale.RX.3: The response should stay within the scope of reviewing this paragraph rather than broader experimentation or literature audit.
rationale.RX.4: Edits or guidance should not distort causal claims or encourage misuse in applied settings.
rationale.RX.5: Observations should be tied to specific phrases/sentences so the author can trace suggestions back to the source text.
